verdict: Northern Star missed FY26 production guidance twice in six months (revised down from 1,700-1,850 koz to 1,600-1,700 koz) while AISC guidance was revised up; the KCGM mill expansion remains the structural thesis but FY26 is a peak-cost year with costs carrying the full weight of the expansion before the throughput benefit is realised.

hypothesis.bull: KCGM mill expansion (26 Mtpa target) delivers 50%+ throughput uplift from FY27; at FY26 AISC guidance midpoint of A$2,750/oz against current spot above A$4,000/oz, group free cash flow is substantial even in the peak-cost year; net cash position of A$293m at December 2025 and no disclosed covenant pressure provide a stable funding platform through the expansion.
hypothesis.bear: Two FY26 production guidance reductions in six months (original 1,700-1,850 koz, revised to 1,600-1,700 koz) and AISC guidance revised above A$2,700/oz indicate execution risk at KCGM and cost pressure at Yandal; if KCGM commissioning extends beyond FY26 or throughput ramp underperforms, the high-cost profile persists into FY27 without the expected production uplift; Pogo (Alaska) adds a small but real USD-denominated jurisdiction and FX exposure.

evidence.label: FY26 production guidance -- revised down twice
evidence.finding: Fact: NST's FY26 group production guidance was originally set at 1,700-1,850 koz; revised to 1,600-1,700 koz as at the December 2025 quarterly. Inference: two downward revisions within one financial year indicate above-expectation operational disruption during the KCGM expansion period.

evidence.label: AISC guidance -- revised up, elevated for FY26
evidence.finding: Fact: FY26 AISC guidance midpoint is approximately A$2,700-2,800/oz; the expansion at KCGM is the primary driver of the elevated cost in FY26 as sustaining and growth capital is at peak during construction. At spot gold above A$4,000/oz, the margin remains material but is compressed relative to a normalised AISC profile.
evidence.source: NST Q2 FY26 Quarterly (December 2025); NST H1 FY26 Half Year Report

evidence.label: KCGM mill expansion -- commissioning underway
evidence.finding: Fact: The KCGM mill expansion at Kalgoorlie is NST's flagship capital project, targeting 26 Mtpa throughput. Commissioning is in progress as at the December 2025 quarterly. Inference: the cost and production disruption in FY26 is attributable to construction; the bull thesis depends on throughput confirmation post-commissioning.
evidence.source: NST Q2 FY26 Quarterly (December 2025); NST H1 FY26 Half Year Report

evidence.label: Balance sheet -- net cash position maintained
evidence.finding: Fact: NST held net cash of A$293m at December 2025. No significant debt maturity pressure disclosed in the corpus. Funding runway is adequate through the KCGM expansion without requiring equity issuance at current gold prices.

evidence.label: AISC margin at spot and stress case
evidence.finding: Fact: At FY26 AISC guidance midpoint of A$2,750/oz and spot gold above A$4,000/oz, the margin is approximately A$1,250/oz. At a stress-case gold price of A$3,500/oz (approximately US$2,200/oz at 0.63), the margin narrows to approximately A$750/oz -- still positive but cash generation is materially reduced. At A$2,200/oz spot equivalent, NST is below breakeven on an AISC basis.
evidence.source: NST Q2 FY26 Quarterly (December 2025) -- inference applied to guidance figures

evidence.label: Yandal -- cost drag on group AISC
evidence.finding: Fact: The Yandal operations (Jundee and Thunderbox) carry a higher per-ounce cost than KCGM due to lower grade and scale. The corpus confirms Yandal's contribution to the elevated group AISC in FY26. Inference: Yandal is a structural drag that persists beyond the KCGM expansion; no asset disposal or strategy change is disclosed.
evidence.source: NST H1 FY26 Half Year Report; NST FY25 Annual Report

evidence.label: Pogo (Alaska) -- jurisdiction and FX exposure
evidence.finding: Fact: NST owns and operates the Pogo underground mine in Alaska, USA. This is the sole non-Australian asset in the portfolio, introducing USD revenue and cost exposure plus US mining jurisdiction risk. Pogo's contribution to group production and AISC is not separately quantified in the corpus query responses.

evidence.label: Ore Reserve life
evidence.finding: Fact: NST's disclosed Ore Reserve life is approximately 13 years, supporting the long-term production profile. Reserve grade of approximately 1.5 g/t is the corpus estimate; the precise JORC breakdown (Measured/Indicated/Inferred) by asset is not separately enumerated in the corpus query responses.
evidence.source: NST FY25 Annual Report -- inference from reserve life disclosure

monitoring_trigger: KCGM mill expansion commissioning confirmation (first throughput rate disclosure at or above 20 Mtpa annualised) or NST May 2026 Ore Reserve and Mineral Resource statement; re-assess immediately if either is released before 30 June 2026. Secondary trigger: gold price below A$3,500/oz sustained for 30 consecutive days -- reassess margin sufficiency at that point.
information_gaps: Hemi Ore Reserve: DEG's Hemi deposit was acquired by NST in May 2025; no Hemi JORC Ore Reserve or development timeline appears in the corpus; Hemi is potentially the most significant organic growth option in the portfolio but is unquantified.
KCGM post-commissioning throughput rate: no confirmed throughput milestone (Mtpa achieved) is in the corpus as at the December 2025 quarterly; bull thesis depends on this confirmation.
Pogo asset metrics: Pogo production, AISC, and reserve life are not separately disclosed in the corpus query responses; the USD cost and revenue exposure is not quantifiable from current sources.

Gold price sensitivity table: no formal scenario analysis at US$2,200/oz, spot, or US$2,800/oz is contained in the corpus; the stress-case margin estimate in the evidence section relies on inference from disclosed AISC guidance.
FY26 second-half production cadence: whether the 1,600-1,700 koz revised guidance is achievable requires H2 FY26 run-rate data not yet in the corpus.
